title: Assertions in Playwright
description: Master assertions and expectations in Playwright tests

questions.question: Which assertion checks if an element is visible?
questions.explanation: toBeVisible() is the correct assertion to check if an element is visible on the page.

questions.question: Complete the assertion to verify page title:
questions.explanation: toHaveTitle() verifies the page title matches the expected value.

questions.question: Playwright assertions automatically retry until the condition is met or timeout occurs.
questions.explanation: Playwright's expect assertions have built-in auto-retry functionality for better reliability.

questions.question: Which assertion verifies the current URL?
questions.explanation: toHaveURL() is used to verify the current page URL matches the expected value.

questions.question: Complete the assertion to check element text:
questions.explanation: toHaveText() verifies exact text content, toContainText() checks if text is included.

questions.question: How do you assert that an element is disabled?
questions.explanation: toBeDisabled() checks if an element has the disabled attribute or state.

questions.question: What's wrong with this assertion?
questions.explanation: Playwright assertions return Promises and must be awaited.

questions.question: The not modifier can be used to negate assertions in Playwright.
questions.explanation: You can use .not before assertions like expect(element).not.toBeVisible() to negate them.

questions.question: Which assertion checks if an input has a specific value?
questions.explanation: toHaveValue() verifies that an input element has the expected value.

questions.question: What does this assertion check?
questions.explanation: toHaveCount() verifies that exactly 3 elements match the locator.
